weekly_summary: This week marks a critical restart. After 8 weeks of dramatic undertraining (1–3 runs/week, 6–24 km), the athlete must re-establish a consistent 4-run rhythm and utilize the full 7-hour weekly budget. Focus is 100% on Zone 2 aerobic base — no intensity yet. Paces should feel conversational (6:00–6:30 min/km based on recent runs). Goal: 32 km, 7 hours, 4 runs. This sets foundation for proper marathon prep.
coaching_notes: Athlete has lost significant training momentum. The next 3 weeks are about rebuilding frequency and trust in the process, NOT intensity. Every run should feel easy and sustainable. Use this week to re-establish the habit of running 4 days/week. Do NOT rush into tempo or long runs — that comes after 3 weeks of consistent base rebuilding. Athlete must also enable HR tracking immediately to get objective data.
recovery_flags: Watch for soreness from increased frequency (jump from 1–2 runs to 4 runs may cause muscle aches)
Keep all runs at conversational pace — no perceived hard effort
If any joint pain appears, revert to 3 runs/week and progress more slowly
next_week_preview: Week 2: Maintain 4 runs at ~34 km (10% increase). Introduce one slightly longer easy run (10–11 km). Still Zone 2 only. Add optional 20-min strength/core 2x/week.

days.title: Rebuild Start – Easy
days.description: Moderate-paced easy run to start the week. Focus on settling into rhythm and enjoying the run. No speed work. Conversational effort.
days.notes: This is a fresh start. Treat it as day 1 of marathon training. Pace ~6:15 min/km. Stop if any sharp pain.

days.title: Recovery + Mobility
days.description: Full rest from running OR optional 20–30 min light yoga/stretching/core work. Focus on hip mobility, glutes, and anterior tibialis. No intense circuit training.
days.notes: Body is adapting to return to 4 runs/week. Foam rolling and dynamic stretching will help. Hydrate well.

days.title: Midweek Easy – Base Consolidation
days.description: Second easy run of the week. Slightly longer than Monday but still well within aerobic base zone. Relax and enjoy the effort. This builds volume without stress.
days.notes: Pace ~6:15 min/km. You should be able to hold a conversation the entire time. If breathing feels strained, slow down.

days.title: Recovery + Strength
days.description: Rest day from running. Optional 20–30 min bodyweight strength: lunges, single-leg deadlifts, planks, glute bridges, calf raises. Build leg stability for marathon demands.
days.notes: Strength 2x/week supports running. Skip if fatigued; running comes first.

days.title: Friday Shakeout – Lighter Easy
days.description: Shorter easy run to prepare body for weekend. Keep effort very controlled — this is priming, not building. Pace should feel effortless.
days.notes: Pace ~6:20 min/km. Very light, conversational. This run comes before Saturday's longer effort.

days.title: Weekend Long Run – Build Duration
days.description: Longest run of the week. This is where aerobic adaptation happens. Start slow, finish strong but controlled. Fuel with water/electrolytes every 30 min on runs >60 min.
days.notes: Pace ~6:15 min/km. This is your first real marathon-building run. Take it seriously but don't race. Fuel strategy: water + simple carbs (gels, sports drink, banana) every 45 min.

days.title: Complete Recovery
days.description: Full rest day. No running, no structured exercise. Focus on sleep, nutrition, hydration, and mobility. Reflect on the week and plan Week 2.
days.notes: Sleep is when adaptation happens. Aim for 8+ hours. Eat protein + carbs within 30 min of Saturday's long run. Hydrate throughout the day. Sunday evening: light 10-min stretching routine.
